CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-27-2015 Off Calendar

Application For Judgment Establishing Title To Real Property Under The Destroyed Records Relife Law [Ccp Sec.751 Et. Seq]
Off Calendar Sep-24-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Charles Crompton, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, Proceedings reported by: Sonia Wong, CSR#11288.

Lee P. Bardellini, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff(s) and Cross-Defendant(s): HAIG G MARDIKIAN, an individual; ADIUVANA-INVEST, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION.
Richard Wallace, Esq., appearing for Defendant(s) and Cross-Complainant(s) FRITZ PROPERTY GROUP, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s): ALL PERSONS UNKNOWN CLAIMING ANY LEGAL OR EQUITABLE RIGHT, TITLE, ESTATE LIEN, OR INTEREST IN THE PROPERTY DESCRIBED IN THIS COMPLAINT ADVERSE TO PLAINTIFFS' TITLE OR ANY CLOUD ON PLAINTIFFS' TITLE THERTO. Default of this Defendants duly entered on July 10, 2015.
No appearance by Defaulted Cross-Defendants: ALL PERSONS CLAIMING ANY INTEREST IN, OR LIEN UPON, THE REAL PROPERTY HEREIN DESCRIBED, OR ANY PART THEREOF. Default of this Cross-Defendants duly entered on August 6, 2015.
Court ordered Defendants Does 1-100 dismissed without prejudice. Court also ordered Cross-Defendants Roes 1 - 50 dismissed without prejudice.
Plaintiff Haig G Mardikian sworn and examined on his own behalf. Ben Ron sworn and examined on behalf of Plaintiff.
Serena Fritz-Cope and Orlando Mayor sworn and examined on behalf of Defendant and Cross-Complainant.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court approves Stipulation for Entry of Judgment of the appearing parties. The Court Enters Judgment against all defaulted parties. The Court will sign Stipulation for Entry of Judgment with Finding of Fact, Conclusions of Law, and Judgment as Exhibit 1 attachment to Stipulated Judgment. See scanned stipulation for entry of judgment.
Court adjourned.

Judgment signed in open court.
